steps.title: Connect songs to practice
steps.content: The songs referenced clam gardens that the Kwakwaka'wakw people used to foster a dietary staple. Archaeological evidence showed that when the practice was prevalent, clam size and abundance grew despite increased harvesting pressure.

steps.title: State what the finding shows
steps.content: Choice A overstates by calling this the defining characteristic of Indigenous music. Choice B speculates about why the songs were made. Choice C invents a species change. The evidence shows effective food-cultivation knowledge preserved in songs. The answer is D.
